calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Closing Of Estate.
Off Calendar Apr-11-2018 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-01-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Ferris for Petitioner.

Mr. Ferris addressed the issue of why the order of October 31, 2016 terminating the conservatorship showed a slightly different amount than what the Estate was appraised at, stated that he filed a supplement, and that the supplement matches the amount in the inventory and appraisal. Mr. Ferris also stated that the bank statement ends June 9, 2017, that the difference between the statement and the cash on hand is for a check that was issued in the amount of $435.00 for the filing fee and a $10.00 fee regarding e file, that his Client lives out of State and that the consultation fee had to do with his Client coming to him with estate planning documents, and that he was not aware that each $5.00 e file charge should have been itemized. The Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-12-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Ferris for (petitioner) Joann Summerville.

The Court stated that more effort is needed to locate Fredericke Fontaine, that it is prepared to grant the petition but prior to distribution the petitioner will need to make more effort to find Ms. Fonataine and if necessary hire a professional to do so. Mr. Ferris stated that the only person with information was the decedents sister who only knew that they went to the same church, that he talked to two heirship companies who said that if there are no ties to the relative and the age of the person is not known there is not enough to go on, that once Ms. Summerville is appointed there will be funds available to pay for a search, that they do not even know if the name is correct and that they do not see how just knowing a name will help. The Court stated that they need to hire a professional and make a more thorough search. The Court granted the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary (subject to review of the order). A status on the close of the estate is set for April 4, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-20-2016 Continued To Oct-04-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
